From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

20th United States national census

| Twentieth census of the United States | |
| --- | --- |
| ---   ← [1970](/wiki/1970_United_States_census "1970 United States census")  **April 1, 1980**  [1990](/wiki/1990_United_States_census "1990 United States census") →   --- | |
| U.S. Census Bureau seal | |
| 1980 U.S. census logo | |
| General information | |
| Country | United States |
| Results | |
| Total population | 226,545,805 (Increase 11.4%) |
| Most populous state | [California](/wiki/California "California") 23,667,902 |
| Least populous state | [Alaska](/wiki/Alaska "Alaska") 401,851 |

The **1980 United States census**, conducted by the [Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), determined the resident population of the United States to be 226,545,805, an increase of 11.4% over the 203,184,772 persons [enumerated](/wiki/Enumeration "Enumeration") during the [1970 census](/wiki/U.S._Census,_1970 "U.S. Census, 1970").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was the first census in which a state—[California](/wiki/California "California")—recorded a population of 20 million people, as well as the first in which all states recorded populations of over 400,000. This was the first census to count [Hispanic and Latino Americans](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") as an ethnicity.

## Census questions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1980_United_States_census&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Census questions")]

The 1980 census collected the following information from all respondents:[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* Address
* Name
* Household relationship
* Gender
* Race
* Age
* Marital status
* Whether of Spanish/Hispanic origin or descent

It was the first census not to ask for the name of the "head of household."[[3]](#cite_note-'70s_246-3)

Approximately 16 percent of [households](/wiki/Households "Households") received a "long form" of the 1980 census, which contained over 100 questions. Full documentation on the 1980 census, including census forms and a procedural history, is available from the [Integrated Public Use Microdata Series](/wiki/Integrated_Public_Use_Microdata_Series "Integrated Public Use Microdata Series").

## Data availability

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1980_United_States_census&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Data availability")]

[Microdata](/wiki/Microdata_(statistics) "Microdata (statistics)") from the 1980 census are freely available through the [Integrated Public Use Microdata Series](/wiki/Integrated_Public_Use_Microdata_Series "Integrated Public Use Microdata Series"). [Aggregate data](/wiki/Aggregate_data "Aggregate data") for small areas, together with electronic boundary files, can be downloaded from the [National Historical Geographic Information System](/wiki/National_Historical_Geographic_Information_System "National Historical Geographic Information System"). [Personally identifiable information](/wiki/Personally_identifiable_information "Personally identifiable information") will be available in 2052.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Between the 1980 census and the 1990 census, the United States population increased by approximately 22,164,837 or 9.8%.
